events.event_summary: Rocket Lab won a launch/satellite deal with Iridium, sending RKLB up ~16% after hours.
primary_drivers: Iridium awarding launch business validates RKLB as a credible medium-lift provider, shifting competitive share in the small/medium launch market
Read-through re-rating of pure-play space/launch peers
Iridium capex commitment signals next-gen constellation refresh cycle

beneficiaries.rationale: Iridium is the customer on the other side of the deal — locking in a launch provider for its next-gen constellation derisks its capex timeline and future service continuity.
Pure-play space peer; RKLB winning a marquee commercial constellation contract lifts the comp multiple for satellite/launch names and validates commercial demand beyond government.
Planet Labs is a small-sat operator that benefits from a more competitive, lower-cost launch market — RKLB's Neutron progress pressures launch pricing for constellation operators.
Redwire is a space infrastructure/components supplier that benefits from increased constellation activity and sentiment read-through on commercial space capex.

losers.rationale: Lockheed has historically been Iridium's satellite bus/integration partner via Thales Alenia; any RKLB share in spacecraft work (not just launch) signals share loss for incumbent primes on commercial constellation refresh.
Boeing's ULA launch JV loses incremental commercial medium-lift business as RKLB's Neutron gets designed in for constellation customers historically served by Atlas/Vulcan or Falcon.

headlines: Rocket Lab’s Iridium Deal Gets Stock Trending After Hours: RKLB Stock Gains

predictions.rationale: Lockheed has historically been Iridium's satellite bus/integration partner via Thales Alenia; any RKLB share in spacecraft work (not just launch) signals share loss for incumbent primes on commercial constellation refresh.
